steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the text. The passage says researchers concluded that 41 settlements were culturally linked to Izapa because each settlement is the same age and configured the same way — pyramid north, plaza south. That causal inference is the central claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Researchers have inferred that Izapa was related to the smaller settlements that surrounded it based in part on the similarity of their construction.': Matches exactly — structural similarity as the basis for inferring cultural connection. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Researchers have determined that the arrangement of Izapa's structures was based on those of other nearby settlements.': Reverses the direction — the passage says the smaller settlements copied Izapa's configuration, not the other way around. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Although archaeologists have learned much about Izapa over years of research, they have only recently found the smaller settlements that surrounded it.': Focuses on the discovery itself rather than the inference about cultural connection, which is the point of the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Cultural ceremonies in Izapa seem to have played a more important role for its residents than those in smaller surrounding settlements did.': The passage never makes a comparative claim about the importance of ceremonies. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
